response.justification: Bogdan is a strong generalist software engineer with 15+ years of experience and genuine staff-level seniority, but falls significantly short of the role's core requirements. The position demands 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions; his AI exposure is confined to his current role at Naver (started April 2025), where he contributes to MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure. Python is entirely absent from his listed skills despite being a primary language requirement. There is no evidence of experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation, or multi-agent frameworks. His background is primarily TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and high-throughput ad-tech pipelines — technically solid, but not aligned with the GenAI delivery depth this staff role demands.
strengths: Staff-level seniority with cross-company technical leadership, mentoring (Refactoring Dojos, Selenium courses), and a track record of owning complex systems end-to-end
Direct hands-on MCP (Model Context Protocol) experience at Naver — one of the key technologies explicitly listed in the job description
Proven data pipeline and high-throughput systems background (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/sec) plus AWS CDK usage, providing a real infrastructure foundation
concerns: Python is absent from his entire resume and skills list despite being the primary language requirement for AI engineering in this role
No demonstrated experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLMs, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.) — the technical core of the position
AI/GenAI experience is extremely recent (current role only, ~1 month as of resume date), nowhere near the 8-year production AI requirement
